mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and lower recession risk, which raises expected earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.25% marks the start of policy liftoff from the zero-rate regime, raising discount rates and tightening financial conditions, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound exit and monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS crossings above 0.25% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or the mean triggered return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS falls over 3 months while PAYEMS rises, household spending is failing to convert job growth into revenue, signaling margin and earnings risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-PAYEMS divergence is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of triggered observations or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO over the prior 3 months signals improving factory output and operating leverage, which supports near-term earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum does not generate higher average 5-day S&P 500 returns than negative momentum in at least two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST crossing below 900 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and cyclical demand, reducing expected earnings for rate-sensitive sectors and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 900 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, the market prices a higher probability of imminent tightening, raising real-rate uncertainty and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with tightening risk
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS condition is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if the interaction coefficient is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth from the prior quarter signals broad demand expansion and rising aggregate profits, which supports equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of observations or if its walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses below 5.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, labor-market slack is scarce but policy is still emergency-easy, increasing liftoff risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor tightening before policy liftoff
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE crossings below 5.5% under FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.
